dialogueChunks.intro: Look at the sky, {player_name}. The green light is gone. The stars are back.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {quest_focus} is sealed again, the corruption is receding, and the river runs clear. {target_npc} went to the temple and said the air felt different: warm, calm. The {enemy_type} are retreating. {quest_threat} has been put to rest at last.
dialogueChunks.outro: You stood against something vast and unknowable and held your ground. {quest_stakes} is over. Take this, and know that this land remembers its defenders.

playerResponses.text: Seeing the stars again is reward enough.
Just make sure nobody breaks those seals again.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... I felt it the moment you completed the ritual. The whole settlement felt it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A warmth spread through {location} like the first day of spring. The blighted crops are still dead, but new shoots push through the blackened soil. {target_npc} says {quest_focus} left an offering at the forest edge: rare herbs arranged in a pattern meaning 'gratitude.' They're thanking us. Thanking you.
dialogueChunks.outro: You healed a wound between the mortal world and {quest_threat}. This is what we can offer. It feels small next to what you did.

playerResponses.text: New shoots through blackened soil. That's the best kind of miracle.
Gratitude from spirits. That's a first for me.
Take care of this land. It nearly died for your ancestors' mistakes.

dialogueChunks.intro: The corruption is gone, but the cost... {player_name}, the cost was heavy.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The temple collapsed during the final ritual. Centuries of knowledge buried. {target_npc} says {quest_focus} chose destruction over the risk of the corruption returning: they sealed the source permanently. The land is healing slowly, but an entire chapter of our history is gone.
dialogueChunks.outro: You did what had to be done against {quest_threat}. Take this: it was found at the edge of the rubble, almost as if it was left for you.

playerResponses.text: Some things are worth more than old stones. Your lives, for instance.
The spirits made a hard choice. So did we. I hope it holds.
